SAP Future of PMM Training
Chapter 4 Learning Guide
futureofpmm.com

Shaping the AI-Mediated Shortlist

Key concepts and takeaways from Chapter 4

Learning Objectives

  • Understand how AI compresses positioning into digestible bullets
  • Craft claims that survive summarization intact
  • Frame competitive differentiation for machine comparison
  • Test your positioning against AI summarization

The Compression Problem

AI agents don't reproduce your messaging — they distill it. In that distillation:

  • Qualifiers and nuance get stripped
  • Emotional language disappears as filler
  • Context-dependent claims get simplified
  • Comparative advantages often get omitted
Key Insight: You're not writing for readers anymore. You're writing for summarizers. The test isn't "does this sound good?" It's "does this survive compression?"

The CRISP Claim Framework

LetterMeaningExample
CConcreteSpecific numbers, names, facts
RRemarkableSomething competitors can't say
IIndependentMakes sense without context
SSpeakableNatural to quote verbatim
PProvableCan be verified by AI

Before & After Positioning

Weak (Gets Compressed Away)Strong (Survives)
"Leading platform with best-in-class capabilities""150+ enterprise data sources with built-in AI governance"
"Innovative solution for modern enterprises""The only platform connecting SAP and non-SAP data in real-time"
"Many Fortune 500 companies trust us""Used by 80% of Fortune 100 retailers"

Competitive Framing Strategies

  1. Reframe the Category — "Unlike traditional ETL tools, Datasphere provides..."
  2. Name Your Advantage — Create terminology for unique capabilities
  3. Quantify Everything — Numbers create comparison points
  4. Emphasize Gaps — Binary differences that competitors can't match

Competitive Content You Need

  • Comparison pages — "[Your Product] vs [Competitor]"
  • Alternative pages — "Best [Competitor] alternatives"
  • Category definitions — Content establishing what criteria matter
  • Migration guides — "Switching from X" signals you're the upgrade

Summarization Stress Test

  1. Feed positioning to multiple AI tools
  2. "Summarize this company's value proposition in 2 sentences"
  3. "Compare [Your Product] to [Competitor]"
  4. "List the 3 main differentiators"
  5. Iterate: Make dropped claims more concrete
Red Flags: If AI says "enterprise platform," "innovative solution," or "helps organizations achieve goals" — your positioning is too generic.

Key Takeaways

  1. AI compresses positioning — write for summarizers, not readers
  2. CRISP claims survive compression
  3. Define comparison criteria, don't just compete on existing ones
  4. Create comparison and alternative pages for AI to reference
  5. Test positioning through AI summarization regularly
